what_it_does: An HTML/CSS desktop mockup of the P3 F4 Activity surface, rendering a live 'now' header derived from system state and a tail of the shared append-only event log, with extensive comments mapping each visual element to real backend features (EVT-now, EVT-stream, EVT-log).
observations: Staging probe not registered in register.json; written only to _panel-staging/.
Top-of-file comment block declares area F, journey-moment ambient, and status staging probe.
Landmark address is ui://activity; EVT-stream and EVT-log have no own address and live inside this region.
Now-view tiles display real now() fields: graph, nodes_total, nodes_resolved, surfaced_pending, presence, mode, last_event.
Presence string is derived by fixed rules, not free text, and mode='off' overrides.
Event stream rows show seq, kind, summary, and a proposed 'face' badge (op/agent/internal).
All event kinds listed are claimed to be real emitted kinds from suite.py; none invented.
Includes live SSE indicator, cursor seq, gapless reconnect, and 15s heartbeat annotations.
The 'face' badge is explicitly flagged as inferred/proposed because append_event currently does not store origin.
Numerous data-ui-ref attributes annotate UI landmarks (ui://activity/* and some ui://canvas/*).
Uses project design-system.css variables for colors, spacing, and radii.
CSS uses a named grid layout (top/rail/canvas/foot/panel) and inline styles for one-off tweaks.
standouts: The mockup is also a specification document: HTML comments bind every element to backend code references.
Explicit honesty about what is real versus inferred/proposed (the face badge).
Activity region replaces the canvas in this probe while still using some ui://canvas/* refs in the side panel.
conventions: Follows project design-system CSS variable naming (--s0, --s1, --acc, --line, etc.).
Uses semantic HTML regions: header, nav, main, footer, aside.
Uses data-ui-ref attributes for UI addressing.
Begins with a structured metadata/probe comment block.
CSS classes are short, lowercase, hyphen-free abbreviations.
concerns: Large inline CSS and HTML comments tightly couple the mockup to specific backend line references that may drift.
The proposed face badge is rendered identically to grounded fields, which could mislead readers who skip the comment.
Some data-ui-ref values in the panel use ui://canvas/* while the main region is ui://activity, creating possible addressing inconsistency.
Staging probe status means this design is not yet integrated or registered.
notes: A heavily annotated desktop mockup that maps the Activity 'now' header and live event stream to real backend mechanisms, while clearly flagging one proposed (not yet implemented) field.
questions: Is the face badge planned to be added to append_event, or is it purely illustrative?
How will the SSE stream be wired into this static mockup in production?
Are the ui://canvas/* data-ui-ref attributes in the side panel intentional cross-region links or leftovers?
What user actions are expected from the top-bar verbs (layers, wire, force, run)?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file is explicitly a staging probe/mockup for the F4 Activity desktop surface and it renders the described now-view plus live event stream.
apparent_themes: Honest grounding: visual design tied to real backend features and data shapes.
Ambient always-on surface: pushed live state rather than polled dashboards.
Shared log: both operator and agent faces write one append-only event log.
Design-as-specification: mockup carries normative comments about what is real versus proposed.
intention_signals: To define the desktop Activity region as a trustworthy, live 'what is happening' operating surface where every visual element can be traced to a real backend field or event kind, and to flag gaps (like event origin/face) that need implementation before the design is real.
novelty: The mockup doubles as a specification document, using HTML comments to explicitly ground each UI element in backend code references and to mark one element (the face badge) as inferred/proposed rather than existing.
contribution: A concrete visual and structural proposal for the Activity desktop region, including its now-view layout, live stream presentation, event-kind color mapping, and a pattern of inline grounding comments that separate real backend fields from proposed ones.
summary_for_embedding: HTML/CSS desktop mockup of the P3 F4 Activity surface showing a derived now-view header and a live tail of the shared append-only event log, with extensive comments grounding elements to backend features and flagging a proposed event-origin face badge.
intention_for_embedding: To specify and visualize an ambient, always-on Activity desktop surface that honestly maps UI elements to real backend state, events, and SSE streaming mechanisms.
